raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint because Barclays failed to resolve a billing/payment dispute from approximately XXXX, and the unresolved disputed balance was later sent or sold to a collection agency. 

I had a Barclays credit card for several years and had a history of making payments on time. Around XXXX, there was a payment/billing error involving approximately {$1000.00}. Barclays appeared to charge or debit me twice, and when the issue was refunded or adjusted, the amount was charged back to the credit card again. This created an incorrect balance that I do not believe I owe. 

Throughout XXXX, I received repeated calls from Barclays about the account. Each time, I explained the situation to the representative. My understanding from those calls was that the representatives could see the duplicate charge/payment/refund issue and told me the matter would be escalated for further review. Despite this, the issue was never corrected or clearly resolved. 

I am now being contacted by XXXX XXXX XXXX regarding an alleged Barclays balance of approximately {$1400.00}. I believe this balance stems from Barclays unresolved billing/payment error, not from valid unpaid purchases. This has also significantly damaged my credit, even though I had previously maintained good credit and did not believe I was delinquent on a legitimate balance. 

I am asking Barclays to fully investigate the XXXX  account activity, including all payments, duplicate charges, refunds, reversals, adjustments, fees, and the decision to send or sell the account to collections. I also request a complete written explanation and account ledger showing how Barclays calculated the alleged balance.
